sections.a_description: Where projects go when no one admits they're dead. Still online, still "in beta," still on your portfolio — but nobody's touched the repo in months.

a_image.alt: Foggy digital graveyard with tombstones shaped like app icons and product logos, dark moody atmosphere

sections.a_description: The diseases that kill projects slowly — or keep them undead.

items.a_description: Not alive, not dead. The domain is renewed, the server is running, and the last commit was eight months ago.

a_image.alt: Zombie hand reaching out of laptop screen in dark room, undead project metaphor, horror atmosphere

items.a_description: "We're still in beta" is the startup version of "it's not you, it's me." The product will never be ready because ready means accountable.

a_image.alt: Door with a forever under construction sign covered in cobwebs, dark abandoned corridor

items.a_description: The hardest question in indie building: is this a problem you can fix, or a project you need to walk away from?

a_image.alt: Fork in a dark road with one path leading to light and the other to darkness, decision crossroads

seo.title: Graveyard — Where Projects Go to Die | Sickness Labs
seo.description: Zombie projects, perpetual beta, pivot or quit. The graveyard is where indie projects go when no one admits they're dead.
